exemplar.title: To what extent does collaboration in Mattel product development play a role in increasing its revenue throughout 2023?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does collaboration in Mattel product development play a role in increasing its revenue throughout 2023?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Frequent and consistent linking of analysis to the key concept of creativity
Strong organizational contextâMattel is central in ratio analysis and Ansoff Matrix discussion
weaknesses: Integration often stays at surface level without defining theoretical dimensions of creativity
Early sections miss explicit links to the key concept, delaying full concept integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-4 performance is clearly demonstrated by solid understanding and recurrent application of creativity to both quantitative and qualitative sections. However, the theoretical underpinning of creativity isnât fully developed and integration isnât seamless from the outset, so 4/5 accurately reflects strong but not fully comprehensive integration.

strengths: Provides the required five supporting documents, all cited via footnotes
Documents chosen are clearly relevant to revenue analyses and collaboration case studies
weaknesses: Most secondary articles lack numerical depth, limiting analytical richness
Range of ideas is adequate but does not explore diverse perspectives beyond core financial data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Quantity requirements are met and sources are used appropriately throughout. Depth is sufficient but not expansive enough for the top bandâs expectation of wide-ranging, in-depth insight, so 3/4 is appropriate.

strengths: Selection combines quantitative ratios with the qualitative Ansoff Matrix, covering multiple analytical dimensions
Tools and theories are correctly applied and linked back to Mattelâs strategic choices
weaknesses: Methodology lacks explicit justification of why these tools best address the research question
Connections to the research question sometimes rely on generic phrasing rather than concrete examples
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While some justification of tool choice is missing and links to the research question could be sharpened, the selection effectively addresses both financial and strategic aspects. The consistent, relevant application of both tools supports a top-band score of 4/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Accurate ratio calculations with data integrated into discussion of strategic outcomes
Analysis consistently ties results back to research question and the role of collaborations
weaknesses: Dataset is limited to two years, preventing broader benchmarking
Some numerical conventions and deeper costâstructure interpretations are underdeveloped
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate moves beyond basic commentary to sustained, evidence-based analysis of financial trends in relation to creative strategy. Despite a narrow data range and minor precision issues, the depth and integration of analysis merit 4/5.

strengths: Conclusions explicitly answer the research question and align with presented data
Key numerical findings are restated clearly, showing consistency with evidence
weaknesses: Limited synthesis of qualitative insights in the concluding narrative
Final paragraph is incomplete and does not fully reconcile earlier data discrepancies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the conclusion could integrate qualitative evidence more fully and requires completion, it delivers a clear, evidence-based answer to the research question, warranting the top 3/3.

strengths: Logical organization with clear title page, contents and labeled sections
Coherent flow guides the reader through methodology, analysis, conclusions
weaknesses: Some table-of-contents entries lack precise page references
Recommendations are placed outside the conclusion, affecting structural clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The commentary exhibits an appropriate structure throughout, fulfilling the highest descriptor for organization, so 2/2 is awarded.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present: title, word count, personal code, citations, bibliography
Bibliography follows IB guidelines and is complete
weaknesses: Citation formats vary between APA and nonstandard styles
Minor inconsistencies in in-text citation styling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor style inconsistencies, the submission meets all IB presentation requirements, earning full marks of 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The commentary follows a logical structure with title page, contents, labelled sections, and coherent flow, matching the descriptor for âappropriate structure throughout.â

Documents used (company financials, corporate press release, trade articles) are clearly relevant to revenue and collaboration; however, most are brief secondary articles and only one provides numerical depth.  Collectively they give adequate but not wide-ranging or deeply analytical coverage, fitting the descriptor of ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than âin-depth and provide range of ideas.â
All required presentation elementsâtitle, word count, personal code, citations, bibliographyâare present as per IB guidelines, earning full marks in this strand.

The student states that five contemporary supporting documents are provided (financial report plus four articles) and cites them throughout.  This meets the 3-5 document requirement and sits at the top band for quantity.

The ratios are calculated correctly and interpreted, and the Ansoff Matrix is discussed in relation to Mattelâs collaborations.  Nonetheless, links to the research question are sometimes generic (âcreative strategyâ) and do not always show how the toolâs outcome informs the final judgment, matching the âadequately appliedâ descriptor where relevance is not always crystal-clear.

Only two years of data are examined, but figures are used correctly in ratio calculations and referenced in discussion, satisfying âadequate selection and use.â  The narrow time frame and absence of broader benchmarking prevent classification as âsufficientâ or âeffective.â

The analysis comments on declining margins and links this to the limited impact of collaboration; however, evaluation remains brief and lacks deeper integration of creativity or cost factors.  This represents âsome analysisâ of the research question rather than mostly effective or thorough analysis.

The student explicitly answers the research question in both the conclusion and throughout the text, stating collaboration did not significantly raise revenue in 2023, meeting the top-band requirement for directly addressing the question.

Two tools/theories are chosenâprofitability ratios and the Ansoff Matrixâcovering both quantitative and qualitative aspects.  This is an adequate, if limited, selection suitable for answering the question but short of an âeffectiveâ breadth expected in the top band.

Conclusions reiterate the numerical findings and align generally with the data presented, but do not synthesise wider evidence (e.g., qualitative insights) deeply.  They are therefore âsomewhatâ consistent rather than fully supported.

Mattel is consistently at the centre of the investigation: financial ratios are calculated from Mattelâs statements, and the Ansoff Matrix is applied specifically to Mattelâs markets and collaborations (e.g., Warner Bros., Barbie movie).  Analysis regularly circles back to the firmâs performance and strategy, demonstrating a strong, though not fully seamless, integration of organisational context across sections.  This aligns with the descriptor for strong analysis with partial integration.

The student repeatedly links the investigation to the key concept of creativity and attempts to frame both the ratio analysis and the Ansoff Matrix discussion around âcreative collaboration.â  Although references to creativity are frequent, the integration is often surface-level (e.g., simply inserting the adjective âcreativeâ before marketing or partnerships).  Still, the commentary shows a solid grasp of how creativity underpins product-development decisions at Mattel and applies the idea in several analytical sections.  This represents strong understanding with partial integration rather than full, seamless integration throughout.

annotations.comment: The inclusion of the word count adheres to IB presentation requirements.
The candidate identifies âCreativityâ as the key concept but does not define its theoretical dimensions or explain why it is central, leading to superficial integration.
The research question is clearly stated and aligns precisely with the investigationâs scope.
Product development recommendations are clear but require concrete case examples to demonstrate real application.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The table of contents is formatted clearly, facilitating navigation through the commentary.
Some TOC entries are ambiguous or missing corresponding page references (e.g., Product Diversification), which may confuse readers.
The Introduction section is clearly demarcated, setting expectations for the reader.
The background on Mattel is informative but does not explicitly link to creativity until later, missing an early concept integration opportunity.
Footnote citations correctly reference supporting documents, enhancing traceability of secondary sources.
The methodology section lists chosen tools but fails to justify why profitability ratios and Ansoff Matrix best address the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Consistent use of supporting document references in footnotes aids clarity and source tracking.
The formula is correct but should be formatted in LaTeX for IB clarity, e.g., $$\frac{Gross\,Profit}{Net\,Sales}\times100\%$$.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The GPM calculation mixes decimal and comma conventions and mislabels the resulting value narrative, reducing precision.
Interpretation links the GPM change to collaborations but omits deeper analysis of cost structures or industry benchmarks.
Recommendations for improving GPM are generic; link them to creative collaboration strategies to enhance relevance.
The NPM formula should be presented as $$\frac{Net\,Income}{Net\,Sales}\times100\%$$ for consistency and clarity.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: NPM interpretation correctly notes the decline but would benefit from discussing how creative cost controls could influence margins.
Ansoff Matrix selection is appropriate but needs explicit justification on how it addresses the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Market penetration strategies list âcreativeâ tactics but lack evidence of impact on 2023 revenue figures.
Diversification tactics align with creativity but are unsupported by any financial or performance data.
The synthesis paragraph rehashes earlier points without offering new insights or integrating findings cohesively.
Conclusion section exists but should concisely summarise both ratio and matrix findings in relation to the research question.
The conclusion states the answer but does not integrate qualitative insights or data breadth to fully justify the claim.
The concluding paragraph trails off mid-sentence; ensure full articulation and completeness.
Recommendations are valuable but appear outside the conclusionâconsider a separate section for improved structure.
Statement that collaboration drives revenue appears inconsistent with earlier data analysis; reconcile or clarify this discrepancy.
The Work Cited is complete and conforms to IB requirements for a separate bibliography.
Citation styles vary between entries (APA, nonstandard formats); standardize formatting per IB citation guidelines.
